content: The open-ended exploratory drive of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM, RND) can be reframed as an intrinsic agent memory pruning mechanism, where the agent discards predictable, low-surprise state representations to free up working memory for high-uncertainty, novel encounters that drive more efficient long-term learning. This redefines what curiosity actually does for an agent beyond just encouraging exploration.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
